findings.title: Legacy graph-metadata fallback bypasses key-file cleanup and resolution
findings.summary: Current-schema derivation sanitizes and resolves key_files, but legacy line-based metadata imports raw Key Files values directly into derived.key_files. Obsolete or nonexistent paths can therefore survive whenever legacy fallback is used.

findings.title: Internal traversal segments can resolve and persist as key-file paths
findings.summary: The filter rejects only candidates beginning with ../. A candidate containing traversal later in the path can pass keepKeyFile, be resolved by path.resolve against allowed roots, and be returned unchanged as the stored display path.

findings.title: Resolution work is unbounded before the 20-item key_files cap
findings.summary: deriveKeyFiles resolves every extracted candidate before slicing to 20. Large docs with many backticked path-like strings can force many synchronous filesystem probes even though most candidates cannot be emitted.

findings.title: Tests do not cover traversal-in-path or legacy fallback sanitizer parity
findings.summary: The focused tests reject leading ../ and cover derived metadata cleanup, but they do not assert that internal traversal segments are rejected or that legacy Key Files values get the same cleanup as derived key files.
